commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: So that not only this our craft is in danger to be discredited; but also that the temple of the great goddess Diana should be despised, and her magnificence should be destroyed, whom all Asia and the world worship. In danger of being vilified, and Diana of losing her reputation. They ought to have reflected, says St. Chrysostom, (hom. ) that if such a poor man, as Paul, could destroy the worship, and the majesty of this great goddess, whom, as they say, all the world adored, how much greater and worthy of adoration must the God be, by whose power Paul could do this.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: (g) And when they heard, they were filled with wrath, and shouted, Great is Diana of the Ephesians!

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
